 There was an issue with removing this item from your saved content. Please try again. 

Deep Dive: Creating Your First Ontology

Learn what the Foundry Ontology is, why it is valuable, and how to build your own. [60 to 90 minutes]

 Register

 Already registered?

 Sign In 

 Cancel 

 Apply 

rate limit

Code not recognized.

 Activate

About this course

 All courses require access to Foundry. If your organization already works with Palantir, contact your administrator for access to your organization-specific Foundry account. Otherwise, eligible individuals can get started by signing-up for a free Developer Tier account here. 

In this course, you will:

Get hands-on with an existing Ontology to better understand what it is and how it makes data easier to use.

See how an Ontology is built and how it provides a common vocabulary to an organization.

Learn how to start building your own Ontology by defining your own object type, relationship, and action

Learn more about how the Ontology lies at the heart of Foundry and how that position impacts how you should think about Foundry-based solutions

This course typically takes about 60 to 90 minutes to complete.

Curriculum 

 Training Overview

 Introduction 

 Permissions & Help 

 Getting Started

 Install the Marketplace Bundle 

 Object Explorer

 Context: Fresh Air 

 Object Explorer 

 Fresh Air Operations 

 Reflections 

 Data Lineage

 Context: Why data lineage matters 

 Data Lineage: Fresh Air pipeline 

 Data Lineage Exploration 

 Reflections 

 Ontology Manager

 Context: Extending Fresh Air's Ontology 

 Ontology Manager 

 Create an object type 

 Create a link type 

 Create an action type 

 Execute your action 

 Reflections 

 Conclusion

 Conclusion

 About this course 

 All courses require access to Foundry. If your organization already works with Palantir, contact your administrator for access to your organization-specific Foundry account. Otherwise, eligible individuals can get started by signing-up for a free Developer Tier account here. 

In this course, you will:

Get hands-on with an existing Ontology to better understand what it is and how it makes data easier to use.

See how an Ontology is built and how it provides a common vocabulary to an organization.

Learn how to start building your own Ontology by defining your own object type, relationship, and action

Learn more about how the Ontology lies at the heart of Foundry and how that position impacts how you should think about Foundry-based solutions

This course typically takes about 60 to 90 minutes to complete.
